name.text: Finding the centre and angle of rotation of a symmetrical layout for a city plaza

questiontext.text:
A designer is creating a symmetrical layout for a city plaza. A decorative stone bench, represented by the segment AB, is rotated around a central fountain O to create a matching bench A'B'.

  • Bench AB: A({@pA[1]@}, {@pA[2]@}) to B({@pB[1]@}, {@pB[2]@})
  • Bench A'B': A'({@pA_prime[1]@}, {@pA_prime[2]@}) to B'({@pB_prime[1]@}, {@pB_prime[2]@})
[[jsxgraph width="450px" height="450px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-10, 10, 10, -10], axis: true, showCopyright: false}); var seg1 = board.create('segment', [{#pA#}, {#pB#}], {strokeColor: 'blue', strokeWidth: 4, name: 'AB', withLabel: true}); var seg2 = board.create('segment', [{#pA_prime#}, {#pB_prime#}], {strokeColor: 'red', strokeWidth: 4, name: "A'B'", withLabel: true}); [[/jsxgraph]]
  1. Use the perpendicular bisector method to find the coordinates of the fountain (Centre O).
    O = [[input:ans1]] [[validation:ans1]] [[feedback:prt1]]
  2. Determine the angle of rotation.
    Angle = [[input:ans2]]° [[validation:ans2]] [[feedback:prt2]]

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

Finding the Centre

We join A to A' and B to B' with dotted lines. The intersection of their perpendicular bisectors identifies the centre of rotation.

[[jsxgraph width="350px" height="350px"]] var b1 = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-10, 10, 10, -10], axis: true, showCopyright: false}); var A = b1.create('point', [{#pA[1]#}, {#pA[2]#}], {name: 'A', color: 'blue'}); var Ap = b1.create('point', [{#ax_p#}, {#ay_p#}], {name: "A'", color: 'red'}); var B = b1.create('point', [{#pB[1]#}, {#pB[2]#}], {name: 'B', color: 'blue'}); var Bp = b1.create('point', [{#bx_p#}, {#by_p#}], {name: "B'", color: 'red'}); b1.create('segment', [A, Ap], {dash: 2, strokeColor: 'gray'}); b1.create('segment', [B, Bp], {dash: 2, strokeColor: 'gray'}); b1.create('perpendicularBisector', [A, Ap], {strokeColor: 'purple'}); b1.create('perpendicularBisector', [B, Bp], {strokeColor: 'orange'}); b1.create('point', [{#cx#}, {#cy#}], {name: 'O', color: 'black', size: 4}); [[/jsxgraph]] Identifying the Angle

By connecting the original point A and its image A' to the centre O, we can measure the rotation angle \(\angle AOA'\).

The rotation for this specific layout is {#ta2#}°.

[[jsxgraph width="350px" height="350px"]] var b2 = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, {boundingbox: [-10, 10, 10, -10], axis: true, showCopyright: false}); // Define key points var O = b2.create('point', [{#cx#}, {#cy#}], {name: 'O', color: 'black', size: 5, fixed: true}); var A = b2.create('point', [{#ax_orig#}, {#ay_orig#}], {name: 'A', color: 'blue', size: 3, fixed: true}); var Ap = b2.create('point', [{#ax_p#}, {#ay_p#}], {name: "A'", color: 'red', size: 3, fixed: true}); // Draw the lines from centre to points to visualize the angle b2.create('segment', [O, A], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'gray', strokeWidth: 2}); b2.create('segment', [O, Ap], {dash: 1, strokeColor: 'gray', strokeWidth: 2}); // Visual Angle arc (ta2 is the angle) b2.create('angle', [Ap, O, A], { radius: 1.5, name: '{#abs(ta2)#}°', withLabel: true, label: {offset: [15, 15]} }); [[/jsxgraph]]

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
